description: Test fixture for Datadog trace query rewrite pipeline. Documents the scenario where metric tag values containing reserved characters are converted to wildcard patterns for trace search queries.

incident_context.issue: Wildcard character '?' replaces reserved characters ('_', '-', '{', '}') in resource names, but patterns fail to match actual trace spans when character counts differ (e.g., metric tag '_post_id' vs trace '{post_id}').

test_cases.description: Metric tag uses underscores where trace resource uses braces around path parameters
test_cases.notes: The '?' wildcard replaces single reserved characters. Pattern fails when '_post_id' (8 chars) maps to '{post_id}' (9 chars) because '?' matches exactly one character.

test_cases.description: Metric tag with hyphen in path segment
test_cases.notes: Hyphen '-' is also replaced by '?' wildcard. In this case 'check-in' matches correctly since hyphen is a single character replacement.

test_cases.description: Multiple path parameters where braces cause character count mismatch
test_cases.notes: Each brace pair '{' and '}' is replaced by a single '?', but the actual trace has both opening and closing braces adding extra characters that '?' cannot account for.

reserved_characters._: Replaced by '?' - used as space separator in metric tags
reserved_characters.-: Replaced by '?' - hyphen in path segments
reserved_characters.{: Replaced by '?' - opening brace for path parameters in trace resource names
reserved_characters.}: Replaced by '?' - closing brace for path parameters in trace resource names

known_issue: When metric tag value uses '_' as a placeholder for characters that map to '{' or '}' in the actual trace resource name, the generated wildcard pattern has a different character count than the real resource name, causing match failures.
